items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》治癒金句整理，「先照顧今天的你」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》有一句被反覆截圖的台詞：
「不要在意別人的目光，
先照顧好你自己。
今天的你要好好的，
才能幫助明天的自己。」

但低潮的人最常做的事，
剛好相反——

你不睡覺，
因為「再撐一下就熬過去了」；
你不吃飯，
因為「胃口不好就不要勉強」；
你不跟朋友說，
因為「不要把負能量倒給別人」。

結果你變成一個
從來不為自己存錢的人。
你把所有力氣都用來「撐過今天」，
沒留半點給明天的自己。

所以明天的你打開眼睛，
發現帳戶是空的，
根本沒有力氣再過一天。

照顧今天的自己，
不是奢侈，
是給明天的你預留下來的存款。

吃一頓正常的飯、
睡一場不滑手機的覺、
出去走一圈不戴耳機——
那不是浪費時間，
是匯款給明天那個還在等你的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是把自己擺到最後一個、最近又開始失眠的朋友——你不是在浪費時間，你是在匯款給明天的自己
variations: 進階版：今晚試一次——關手機、洗澡、好好睡。明天起床看看那個你，會不會比連續三天硬撐的版本，多一點點力氣

en.content:
A line from Doctor Slump that keeps getting screenshotted:
"Stop worrying about how others see you.
Take care of yourself first.
Today's you has to be okay
for tomorrow's you to have a chance."

But what people in a slump do
is the exact opposite —

You don't sleep,
because "just push through one more night."
You don't eat,
because "no appetite, don't force it."
You don't tell your friends,
because "don't dump negativity on people."

You turn into someone
who never saves anything for themselves.
You spend everything just surviving today,
leaving nothing for tomorrow's you.

So tomorrow's you opens their eyes,
finds the account empty,
and has nothing left to face another day.

Taking care of today's you
isn't a luxury.
It's a deposit you're leaving for tomorrow.

A real meal,
a full night of sleep without scrolling,
a walk outside with no earbuds —
that isn't wasted time.
It's a wire transfer to tomorrow's you,
who's still waiting for you to show up.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always puts themselves last and just started losing sleep again — you're not wasting time, you're wiring funds to tomorrow's you
variations: Advanced: try it tonight — phone off, real shower, real sleep. Look at the version of you that wakes up tomorrow vs. the version that pushed through three nights raw. The difference is the receipt

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒系韓劇台詞推薦，「你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句被瘋狂截圖的台詞：
「你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？」

但奇怪的是，
大多數人從來不問自己這個問題。

你會問朋友「我這份工作該不該辭？」
你會問家人「我這個對象適不適合？」
你會問IG的算命帳號「我什麼時候會遇到對的人？」

你問了一圈，
收到十種版本的答案，
結果還是回到原點：
你還是不知道自己要什麼。

那是因為從一開始，
答案就不在他們嘴裡。

他們可以告訴你他們的版本，
卻沒辦法替你過你的日子。
他們不會在你凌晨三點失眠的時候陪你坐著，
也不會在你決定錯了的時候替你承擔。

下一次想問別人之前，
先安靜地問自己一句：
「如果沒有人會評價我，
我真的想要哪一個？」

那個答案，才是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是徵詢一堆人意見、最後反而更迷惘的朋友——別人的版本永遠不會是你的人生
variations: 進階版：今天起把「我該怎麼辦」改成「我想要什麼」——前者讓別人主導，後者把方向盤拿回來

en.content:
A K-drama line that gets screenshotted on loop:
"Your happiness — why are you asking other people?"

The weird part is,
most people never ask themselves that.

You ask a friend, "Should I quit this job?"
You ask family, "Is this person right for me?"
You ask a tarot account on Instagram, "When will I meet the one?"

You collect ten different answers,
and end up exactly where you started:
still not knowing what you actually want.

That's because the answer
was never in their mouths to begin with.

They can tell you their version,
but they can't live your days for you.
They won't sit with you at 3 AM when you can't sleep,
or carry the weight when the choice turns out wrong.

Before you ask someone else next time,
ask yourself this quietly:
"If no one was going to judge me,
which one would I actually pick?"

That answer is yours.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who polls everyone and ends up more lost — other people's versions will never be your life
variations: Advanced: starting today, swap 'what should I do' for 'what do I want' — the first hands the wheel over, the second takes it back

items.sourceNote: KORB《戀愛的實用指南》2026年金句整理，「以為到了某個時間就能戀愛」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《戀愛的實用指南》有一句台詞戳到很多人：
「我以為時機到了，
隨時都可以開始談戀愛——
結果我把最好的那幾年，
全部浪費掉了。」

你二十三歲覺得「還太早，先拚事業」，
二十七歲覺得「還沒準備好，再等一下」，
三十一歲覺得「現在認真起來太累，隨緣」，
三十五歲突然發現：
你已經習慣一個人吃飯、一個人旅行、
一個人在家把熱湯喝完。

不是你不會愛人，
是你太久沒練習，
連「對某個人感興趣」都需要重新學。

戀愛從來不是一個按鈕，
按下去就會啟動。

它是一種肌肉。
你太久沒用它，
就會慢慢萎縮——
直到你想用的那一天，
才發現它不聽你的話。

所以那句話的意思不是叫你慌張地隨便愛，
是提醒你：
你不練習感受，
感受就會慢慢離你而去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是說「現在還不是時候」的朋友——感情這件事，越等越不會
variations: 進階版：今天先做小事——對一個你覺得有趣的人多回兩句話，那不是戀愛，那是讓那塊肌肉開始動的暖身

en.content:
A line from The Practical Guide to Love that lands hard:
"I thought I could fall in love
any time the timing felt right —
turns out I've already wasted
the best years on waiting."

At 23 you said "too early, focus on the career."
At 27 you said "not ready yet, give it a minute."
At 31 you said "too tired to start now, whatever happens happens."
At 35 you suddenly realize:
you've gotten used to eating alone,
traveling alone,
finishing a hot bowl of soup alone at home.

It's not that you can't love someone.
It's that you've been out of practice so long
even "being interested in a person"
feels like a skill you forgot.

Love was never a button
that starts when you press it.

It's a muscle.
Don't use it long enough,
and it slowly atrophies —
so the day you finally want to use it,
it stops listening to you.

That line isn't telling you to panic-love anyone.
It's a reminder:
if you don't practice feeling,
feeling slowly leaves you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'not the right time' — this kind of muscle weakens the longer you wait
variations: Advanced: do something small today — reply two extra lines to someone interesting. That isn't love. That's the muscle starting to move again

items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》「有悲傷的我就會有幸福的我」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》有一句被反覆引用的台詞：
「雖然今天很幸福，
明天也可能會忽然變得悲傷，
但是沒關係——
有悲傷的我，就會有幸福的我。」

大多數人聽到「悲傷」這兩個字，
第一反應是想立刻把它趕走。

你會跟自己說：
「不要再難過了，
振作一點，
別人比你慘多了。」

但這句話真正在說的是：
悲傷不是一個你需要解決的故障。
悲傷是你還能感受的證據。

一個從不悲傷的人，
通常也不太會真正快樂——
他只是把所有開關都關掉了。

你願意為一段關係難過、
為一個夢想破碎心痛、
為一段日子的結束哭出來，
那都不是你的弱點。

那是你還沒被生活磨成一塊石頭的證據。

下次悲傷找上你的時候，
不要那麼急著趕它走。
它來，是來告訴你：
你還活著、還在乎、還沒放棄感受。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明很難過、卻一直跟自己說「我應該振作」的朋友——你不需要趕走悲傷，悲傷是你還活著的證明
variations: 進階版：今天試一次——感覺到悲傷時，不要立刻找事情分散注意力。坐下來，看它五分鐘。你會發現它其實沒有你想像的那麼可怕

en.content:
A line from Doctor Slump that keeps coming back:
"Today might be happy.
Tomorrow might suddenly turn sad.
But that's okay —
a me who can be sad
is also a me who can be happy."

Most people hear the word 'sad'
and immediately try to chase it away.

You tell yourself:
"Stop being upset.
Pull it together.
Other people have it worse."

But what that line is really saying:
sadness isn't a malfunction to fix.
Sadness is proof you can still feel.

A person who never feels sad
usually doesn't feel real joy either —
they just turned all the switches off.

The fact that you can grieve a relationship,
ache when a dream falls apart,
cry when a chapter ends —
that isn't weakness.

That's proof life hasn't worn you down into stone yet.

Next time sadness comes for you,
don't rush so hard to push it out.
It's here to tell you:
you're alive, you still care, you haven't stopped feeling.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps telling themselves 'I should pull it together' while clearly hurting — you don't need to evict sadness, it's the proof you're still alive
variations: Advanced: try this once — when sadness shows up, don't immediately distract yourself. Sit down, look at it for five minutes. You'll find it's smaller than it looked

items.sourceNote: 少女心文室韓劇台詞整理，「家裡有人等著你」溫暖主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一種被反覆寫進台詞的設定：
「不管在外面受了多少委屈，
只要想到家裡還有人等著你、
愛著你，
就好像又有力氣面對明天。」

但很多人看到這句話，
第一個感覺不是溫暖，
是空。

因為你下班回家，
打開門，
沒有人問你今天怎麼樣，
沒有人記得你昨天說過今天有重要會議，
甚至沒有一盞燈是替你留的。

但這句話真正想告訴你的，
從來不是「你必須有那個家」。

是：
你需要在你的人生裡，
建立一個地方——
回去的時候會被理解、被記得、被等著。

那個地方不一定是血緣，
可以是一個朋友的訊息、
一個固定週四的飯局、
一個會記得你不喜歡香菜的人。

所謂「家」，
從來不是一棟房子。

是這個世界上至少有一個座標，
可以讓你在崩潰的那一天，
知道往哪裡走。

如果你還沒有那個座標，
別放棄找——
找到一個，這個世界就不會那麼冷。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個過年回家覺得很累、平常下班又覺得房子很空的朋友——「家」不是地址，是有人記得你的座標
variations: 進階版：這週主動約一個讓你覺得安心的人吃一頓飯。不要找理由，就說「最近想見你」。那一頓飯，就是你在替自己造一個座標

en.content:
There's a setup K-dramas keep returning to:
"No matter how rough the world is to you,
the thought that someone's waiting at home,
someone loves you,
is what gives you strength for tomorrow."

A lot of people read that
and the first thing they feel isn't warmth.
It's emptiness.

Because you get home from work,
open the door,
and no one asks how today went.
No one remembered you said you had a big meeting yesterday.
Not even a single light was left on for you.

But what that line is actually saying
was never "you must have that family."

It's:
you need a place in your life —
somewhere you go back to
and are understood, remembered, waited for.

That place doesn't have to be blood.
It can be one friend's text thread,
a standing Thursday dinner,
someone who remembers you don't eat cilantro.

'Home' was never a building.

It's having at least one coordinate in this world
so that on the day you fall apart,
you know which direction to walk.

If you don't have that coordinate yet,
don't stop looking —
find one, and the world stops being so cold.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels exhausted going home for holidays and finds the apartment empty after work — 'home' isn't an address, it's a coordinate where someone remembers you
variations: Advanced: this week, message one person who makes you feel safe and ask them to dinner. No reason needed — just 'I wanted to see you.' That meal is you building a coordinate of your own

items.sourceNote: KORB《Sold Out On You》台詞整理，「相信命運就明天再見」浪漫場景延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一種讓人臉紅的台詞模板：
「如果你相信命運，
那我們明天，
就在這裡再見一次吧。」

聽起來很美，
但你仔細想會發現——
這句話真正的重點不是「命運」，
是那個人願意把明天的自己交出來。

「相信命運」是一種包裝，
核心其實是：
我願意冒一次險，
讓你決定我們有沒有以後。

現實裡比較常聽到的版本是：
「再說啦」、
「看狀況」、
「最近有點忙」。

沒有命運，
只有不夠想見你。

下次有人說「再聯絡」，
你就知道——
那不是命運在等你，
是對方還沒決定要不要把明天交給你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在等對方主動聯絡、把「順其自然」當答案的朋友——「相信命運」常常是「我還沒決定」的禮貌說法
variations: 進階版：你不需要等命運。如果你想見一個人，就直接約一個時間、一個地點。命運會的事情，你自己也可以做

en.content:
K-dramas have this line that makes everyone blush:
"If you believe in fate,
let's meet here again tomorrow."

It sounds beautiful,
but think about it for a second —
the real point isn't 'fate.'
It's that the person is willing to hand over their tomorrow.

'Believing in fate' is the wrapping.
The core is:
I'm willing to take a risk
and let you decide if we have a future.

The real-world version usually sounds like:
"We'll see."
"Maybe later."
"Things are kind of busy."

There's no fate.
There's just 'not wanting to see you enough.'

Next time someone says 'let's keep in touch,'
you'll know —
that's not fate waiting on you.
It's the other person not yet deciding to give you their tomorrow.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps waiting for the other person to reach out, treating 'whatever happens, happens' as an answer — 'believing in fate' is often the polite version of 'I haven't decided yet'
variations: Advanced: you don't have to wait on fate. If you want to see someone, pick a time and a place. The things fate is supposed to do — you can do them yourself

items.sourceNote: POPBEE 2026治癒韓劇台詞推薦，「你的幸福為什麼要問別人」主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
有一句被反覆引用的韓劇台詞：
「你的幸福，
為什麼要問別人？」

第一次聽會覺得，
啊這句話好對。

第二次聽會發現，
你每天都在問。

買一件衣服要先問朋友適不適合、
離職前要先問家人會不會反對、
搬出去住前要先問男友能不能接受、
甚至連點哪一杯咖啡都要看店員臉色。

你以為你在「徵詢意見」，
其實你在「外包人生」。

問別人沒有錯，
錯的是你不問自己。

你連自己想要什麼都不知道，
別人怎麼可能告訴你你會不會幸福？

從今天開始，
做一個決定之前，
先問自己一句：
「如果沒有別人的意見，
我會選哪一個？」

那個答案，
才是你真正想要的人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個做決定永遠先問十個人意見、最後選了一個自己也不喜歡的選項的朋友——別人的意見可以參考，但你的人生不能外包
variations: 進階版：今天試一次，做一個小決定不要問任何人。買午餐、選電影、決定週末去哪——只用自己的答案。練習久了，大決定也會變得不那麼可怕

en.content:
There's a K-drama line people keep coming back to:
"Your happiness —
why are you asking other people about it?"

First time you hear it, you nod.
Yeah, that's right.

Second time, you realize
you ask other people every single day.

You ask a friend if the shirt suits you,
ask family before quitting your job,
ask your partner before moving out,
ask the barista with your eyes
before picking the coffee.

You think you're 'gathering input.'
You're actually outsourcing your life.

Asking isn't the problem.
Not asking yourself is.

If you don't know what you want,
how could anyone else
tell you whether you'll be happy?

Starting today,
before any decision,
ask yourself first:
"If no one had an opinion,
which would I pick?"

That answer
is the life you actually want.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who polls ten people for every decision and ends up choosing the option none of them — including themselves — actually wanted. Other people's input is fine, but you can't outsource your life
variations: Advanced: today, make one small decision without asking anyone. Lunch, movie, weekend plan — your answer only. Practice on small ones, and the big ones get less scary

items.sourceNote: Vogue HK《低谷醫生》16句金句整理，「現在不順不會一輩子不順」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》裡有一句反覆出現的話：
「沒事的，
我們只是現在不順，
不會一輩子都不順。
抬頭挺胸，
很快就會有好事發生。」

你正在低潮的時候聽，
會覺得這句話有點輕。

畢竟你連起床都很難，
誰跟你講「抬頭挺胸」？

但這句話的關鍵，
從來不是「立刻好起來」，
是那個「現在」。

「現在」這個詞很重要，
因為它意味著有「以後」。

你現在不順，
不代表你的人生不順。
你現在很累，
不代表你接下來十年都會這麼累。
你現在愛的人沒有愛你，
不代表沒有人會愛你。

低潮的時候最容易犯的錯，
就是把「現在」當成「永遠」。

但季節都會換，
冬天再長都會結束，
你也是。

你不需要強迫自己抬頭挺胸，
你只需要記得：
這是「現在」，
不是「永遠」。

撐過這段，
你會發現自己其實一直在走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在低潮、覺得自己永遠走不出來的朋友——你只是「現在」不順，不是「永遠」不順，這兩個字差別很大
variations: 進階版：今晚睡前想一件三年前讓你覺得「我撐不過去了」的事——你撐過來了。那件事現在連名字都記不太清楚。三年後的你，看現在也會這樣

en.content:
In Doctor Slump, this line keeps coming back:
"It's okay.
We're just having a rough patch right now.
It's not going to last forever.
Keep your head up —
something good is coming."

When you're actually in the slump,
this line lands a little light.

Getting out of bed is already hard.
Who's got 'head up' energy?

But the real key in that line
isn't 'cheer up immediately.'
It's the words 'right now.'

'Right now' matters,
because it implies a 'later.'

You're struggling right now,
not for the rest of your life.
You're tired right now,
not for the next ten years.
The person you love right now doesn't love you back —
that doesn't mean no one will.

The most common mistake in a slump
is treating 'right now'
as 'forever.'

Seasons change.
Even the longest winter ends.
So do you.

You don't have to force yourself
to keep your head up.
You just have to remember:
this is 'right now,'
not 'forever.'

Get through this stretch
and you'll realize
you've been walking the whole time.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend in a slump who feels they'll never come out of it — it's only 'right now,' not 'forever.' Those two words make all the difference
variations: Advanced: tonight before bed, think of something three years ago that felt like 'I can't survive this' — you survived. You can barely remember the details now. Three years from now, you'll look at this the same way

items.sourceNote: TVBS女人我最大《今生也請多指教》15句台詞整理，「人到死都不會懂事」主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句被低估的台詞：
「人到死都不會懂事，
大家都是裝出來的。」

第一次聽會笑，
第二次聽會鬆一口氣。

因為你以為「長大」是會在某一天突然完成的事——
某天醒來，
你會懂得處理人際關係、
會自然管理財務、
會在家人吵架的時候從容打圓場、
會對所有事情都有答案。

但你三十歲了，
四十歲了，
還是經常覺得自己在裝。

你在公司裝有條理、
在朋友面前裝沒事、
在父母面前裝放心、
在另一半面前裝堅強。

你以為大家都已經懂了，
只有你還在裝。

但真相是：
所有人都在裝。

那個看起來最有條理的同事、
那個看起來婚姻很美滿的朋友、
那個看起來什麼都不怕的父母——
他們也都在裝，
只是裝得比較久，
所以裝得比較順。

所以下次你覺得「我怎麼還沒長大」，
記得這句話：
沒有人真的長大，
大家只是學會了，
在沒有準備好的時候，
還是把該做的事做完。

那不是裝，
那叫成熟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個經常覺得「別人都很有條理只有我在亂」的朋友——所有人都在裝，差別只在於裝多久而已
variations: 進階版：下次有人在你面前看起來游刃有餘，記得他可能也是早上才哭過、出門前才崩潰過。你看到的是他選擇給你看的版本

en.content:
There's a K-drama line that doesn't get enough credit:
"Nobody actually grows up before they die.
Everyone's just pretending."

First time you hear it, you laugh.
Second time, you exhale.

Because you used to think
'becoming an adult' would happen on a specific day —
you'd wake up
knowing how to handle relationships,
manage money instinctively,
smoothly defuse family arguments,
have the answer to every question.

But you're thirty,
you're forty,
and you still feel like you're faking it.

Faking competence at work,
faking 'I'm fine' with friends,
faking 'don't worry about me' with parents,
faking strong with your partner.

You assume everyone else figured it out
and you're the only one still pretending.

The truth is:
everyone's pretending.

The coworker who looks the most put-together,
the friend whose marriage looks perfect,
the parents who seem afraid of nothing —
they're all pretending too.
They've just been pretending longer,
so they got smoother at it.

So next time you think
'why haven't I grown up yet,'
remember:
no one really grows up.
They just learn
to get the thing done
even when they weren't ready.

That's not pretending.
That's being an adult.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's convinced everyone has it together except them — everyone is faking it. The only difference is how long they've been doing it
variations: Advanced: next time someone looks like they have it all under control, remember — they might have cried that morning or fallen apart before walking out the door. You're seeing the version they chose to show you

items.sourceNote: POPBEE《淚之女王》10句經典台詞整理，「愛不是只有甜蜜，是一起撐過痛苦」主題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》裡有一句很多人忽略的台詞：
「愛不是只有開心地說甜言蜜語，
愛是一起忍受痛苦，
是選擇留下來而不是跑掉。」

第一次聽會覺得，
好像有點沉。

但你想想就會發現，
所有看起來「很甜」的關係，
都是經歷過幾次「想跑」之後
還是選擇留下來的。

沒有人天生就會愛，
大家都是練習出來的：
練習在對方很煩的時候不爆走、
練習在自己很累的時候還願意聽他講話、
練習在吵架後先低頭、
練習在很想分手的那天又留下來吃一頓飯。

甜不是愛的證據，
「留下來」才是。

所以下次你又懷疑「我們是不是不夠愛了」，
問自己一個問題：
上一次很想跑的時候，
我為什麼留下來了？

那個答案，
就是愛還在的證明。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在懷疑自己感情是不是冷掉、開始覺得「不夠甜」的朋友——甜是浪漫，留下來才是愛
variations: 進階版：今晚問另一半一個問題：「你上一次很想離開的時候是什麼時候？」如果他願意誠實回答，那就是你們關係夠穩的證明——能談「想離開」的關係，才是真的留下來

en.content:
There's a line from Queen of Tears people often overlook:
"Love isn't just being happy and saying sweet things.
Love is enduring pain together.
It's choosing to stay
instead of running."

First time you hear it,
it sounds a little heavy.

But think about it for a second:
every relationship that looks 'so sweet'
is actually one where someone wanted to run
several times — and chose to stay.

No one is born knowing how to love.
Everyone practices:
practicing not exploding when they're annoying,
practicing still listening when you're exhausted,
practicing being the first to soften after a fight,
practicing one more dinner on the day you almost walked out.

Sweet isn't proof of love.
'Stayed' is.

So next time you wonder
'are we not in love anymore,'
ask yourself:
the last time I wanted to run,
why did I stay?

That answer
is the proof love is still there.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend questioning their relationship because it's not 'sweet enough' anymore — sweet is romance, but staying is love
variations: Advanced: tonight, ask your partner: 'When was the last time you wanted to leave?' If they answer honestly, that's proof the relationship is solid — a relationship where you can talk about 'wanting to leave' is one that actually stayed

items.sourceNote: 鬼怪經典台詞「跟你在一起的時光都很耀眼」整理，日常感官浪漫延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《鬼怪》裡有一句被引用到爛的台詞：
「跟你在一起的時光都很耀眼，
因為天氣好、
因為天氣不好、
因為天氣剛剛好——
每一天都很美好。」

聽起來很浪漫，
但你冷靜想想，
這句話真正厲害的地方，
不是天氣，
是「每一天」。

大部分的關係，
好的時候你覺得很愛、
累的時候你開始懷疑、
吵架的時候你想分開。

你的「愛」是有條件的，
要對方體貼、要天氣好、要心情對。

但這句台詞的設定是：
不管天氣怎樣、不管那一天發生什麼，
只要是「跟你」，
那一天就成立了。

這不是浪漫，
這叫做選擇。

你選擇了一個人之後，
你的好天氣壞天氣、
你的好心情壞心情，
都會因為他的存在而成立。

所以下次有人對你說
「跟你在一起好像每天都很好」，
那不是嘴甜，
那是他已經做完那個選擇了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等「對的人會讓我每天都很開心」的朋友——對的人不是讓你每天都開心，是讓你每一天都「成立」
variations: 進階版：你最近的好天氣，是因為天氣，還是因為某個人？如果是後者，那個人值得你今晚發一則訊息——告訴他這件事，他不會知道，除非你說

en.content:
There's a Goblin line that's been quoted to death:
"Every moment with you was dazzling.
Because the weather was good.
Because the weather was bad.
Because the weather was just right.
Every single day was beautiful."

It sounds romantic,
but the real power of the line
isn't the weather.
It's 'every single day.'

In most relationships,
when things are good you feel in love,
when you're tired you start to doubt,
when you fight you want to leave.

Your love is conditional:
they have to be thoughtful,
the weather has to cooperate,
your mood has to be right.

But the line's premise is:
no matter the weather,
no matter what happens that day,
if it's 'with you,'
the day counts.

That's not romance.
That's a choice.

Once you've chosen someone,
your good and bad weather,
your good and bad moods,
all start to count
because they're in it.

So next time someone says
'every day with you feels good,'
that's not flattery.
They've already made the choice.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting for 'the right person who'll make every day happy' — the right person doesn't make every day happy, they make every day count
variations: Advanced: was your good day today because of the weather, or because of someone? If it's the second one, that person deserves a text tonight — tell them. They won't know unless you say it

items.sourceNote: 《愛的迫降》「任何事拖久了都不好，無論是人還是愛情」經典台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《愛的迫降》有一句被很多人記住的台詞：
「任何事拖久了都不好，
無論是人還是愛情都一樣。」

第一次聽會覺得，
有道理但有點殘忍。

第二次聽你會想到一個人。
那個你拖了三個月還沒回的訊息、
那個你拖了半年還沒確認的關係、
那個你拖了兩年還沒決定要不要在一起的對象。

你以為「再等等」是溫柔，
其實「再等等」是另一種傷害。

你拖著對方等一個你自己都不確定的答案，
你拖著自己等一個你早就知道結果的決定。

拖越久，
喜歡的會變成不甘心，
不甘心的會變成怨。

所以這句台詞真正在說的是：
如果你已經知道答案，
就不要拖。

答應或拒絕、
留下或離開、
說愛或說再見——
越早做完，傷害越小。

拖延不是溫柔，
是把痛苦分期付款給對方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明知不愛但還在拖、或是明知該離開但還在留的朋友——拖延不是善意，是把痛苦延長
variations: 進階版：今晚做一個決定——那個你已經想了三個月的答案，現在就傳出去。痛一次比痛三個月好

en.content:
There's a Crash Landing on You line a lot of people remember:
"Dragging anything out is bad —
whether it's a person
or a relationship."

First time you hear it,
it sounds true but a little brutal.

Second time, someone comes to mind.
The message you've left unread for three months.
The relationship you haven't defined in half a year.
The person you've been not-deciding about for two years.

You thought 'let me think a bit more' was kind.
It's actually another kind of damage.

You're making them wait on an answer
you aren't even sure of.
You're making yourself wait on a decision
you already know.

The longer you drag it,
the more 'like' becomes 'unwilling to let go,'
and 'unwilling to let go' becomes resentment.

What the line is really saying:
if you already know the answer,
don't drag it.

Yes or no.
Stay or go.
'I love you' or 'goodbye.'
The sooner you finish it,
the smaller the damage.

Dragging it out isn't being kind.
It's putting the other person on a pain payment plan.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who knows they don't love them but keeps dragging it out, or knows they should leave but keeps staying — dragging it isn't kindness, it's prolonging the pain
variations: Advanced: tonight, make a decision — the answer you've been holding for three months, send it now. Hurting once is better than hurting for three months

items.sourceNote: Beauty321「20句韓劇心動表白台詞」、「因為我們在一起完整了這個世界」延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一種台詞最容易讓人融化：
「因為我們在一起，
才完整了這個世界。」

聽起來很浪漫，
但這句話被誤會了很久。

大部分人聽到「完整」就以為，
那個對的人會把你「補滿」——
你內心的缺口、你過去的傷、
你那些連自己都不喜歡的部分，
都會被他治好。

所以你拼命找那個能「補你」的人。
找了十年，
發現每個人最後都讓你更空。

但這句台詞的本意，
從來不是「他補你」、
也不是「你補他」。

是「一起，世界才完整」。

你還是不完整、
他也還是不完整——
但你們把彼此的不完整放在一起，
那個世界就剛剛好。

所以不要再等那個「完整你的人」了，
等下去你會錯過所有人。

你要找的是
那個願意跟你「一起不完整」的人。
他知道你有缺口、
你知道他有缺口，
你們不假裝、不修補，
就這樣放在一起。

那才是這句台詞真正的意思。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在等「對的人會讓我變完整」的朋友——沒有人能讓你變完整，你只能找一個願意跟你一起不完整的人
variations: 進階版：今晚列三個你覺得自己「不夠好」的地方，然後想想——身邊有沒有一個人，知道這三件事還是留下來？如果有，那個人就是答案

en.content:
K-dramas have a kind of line that melts everyone:
"Because we're together,
the world became complete."

It sounds romantic,
but this line has been misunderstood for a long time.

Most people hear 'complete' and think
the right person will 'fill you in' —
your gaps, your past wounds,
the parts of yourself you don't like,
will all be healed by them.

So you spend years searching
for the person who'll 'complete' you.
Ten years later,
everyone has just left you emptier.

But the line never meant
'they complete you'
or 'you complete them.'

It meant 'together, the world becomes complete.'

You're still not whole.
They're still not whole.
But you put your incompleteness
next to theirs,
and somehow the world fits.

So stop waiting
for the person who'll 'make you whole.'
If you keep waiting, you'll miss everyone.

What you're looking for
is the person willing to be 'incomplete with you.'
They know you have gaps.
You know they have gaps.
No pretending, no fixing.
Just placed next to each other.

That's what the line actually means.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting for 'the right person who'll make me whole' — no one can complete you. You can only find someone willing to be incomplete alongside you
variations: Advanced: tonight, list three things about yourself you think 'aren't good enough,' then ask — is there someone in your life who knows all three and still stayed? If yes, that's the answer

items.sourceNote: POPBEE《淚之女王》10句經典台詞整理，婚姻與愛情命題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》裡有一個被很多人忽略的細節：
他們吵到最兇的那一場，
沒有人說「我要離婚」，
說的是「你還記不記得我們最一開始的樣子」。

這句話比所有甜言蜜語都殘忍。

因為「記不記得」的意思是，
我現在已經快不記得了。

婚姻裡最危險的，
從來不是大吵一架，
是某一天你發現——
你連對方原本喜歡你哪一點都想不起來。

甜的時候，
你以為「我愛你」是一句話；
累的時候，
你才知道「我愛你」是一個工作。

要每天上班、不能請假、
沒有年終、偶爾還要加班。

那些撐過去的人不是比較幸運，
是他們願意每天打卡。

所以下次你跟某個人說「我愛你」的時候，
你可以順便問問自己：
明天你還願意打卡嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在猶豫要不要繼續這段關係的朋友——愛不是感覺，是一份願不願意每天打卡的工作
variations: 進階版：今晚回想一下你跟對方「最一開始」的樣子，如果你還想得起來——恭喜你，這段關係還有救；如果想不起來，那也是一個答案

en.content:
There's a detail in Queen of Tears most people missed:
in their worst fight,
no one said "I want a divorce."
What they said was
"do you still remember
what we were like at the start?"

That line is more brutal than any insult.

Because "do you remember"
means
I'm already starting to forget.

The most dangerous thing in a marriage
isn't a huge fight.
It's the day you realize
you can't remember
what they originally liked about you.

When things are sweet,
you think "I love you" is a sentence.
When things are tired,
you learn that "I love you" is a job.

Daily clock-in. No PTO.
No year-end bonus.
Sometimes overtime.

The people who make it aren't luckier.
They just keep showing up to work.

So next time you say "I love you,"
ask yourself quietly:
are you still clocking in tomorrow?
en.editorNote: Send to the friend debating whether to stay in the relationship — love isn't a feeling, it's a job. The question is whether you're still clocking in tomorrow
variations: Advanced: tonight, try to remember what the two of you were like at the very start. If you can — good news, the relationship still has a chance. If you can't, that's also an answer

items.sourceNote: Tatler Asia〈2023年韓劇15大超寫實金句〉《絕世網紅》睡眠與順遂台詞延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
有一句韓劇台詞看似在罵人，
其實在點醒人：
「發生這點事就睡不著，
看來你之前過得還蠻順遂的。」

第一次聽你會生氣，
第二次聽你會沉默，
第三次聽你會笑出來——
因為你發現，是真的。

我們這代人最大的問題不是吃苦，
是「沒吃過苦」的人開始遇到苦。

小時候被保護得很好、
讀書的時候照著SOP走、
畢業前以為人生有一個固定的下一步。

結果某一天，
工作出包、感情出事、家裡出狀況——
你發現原來人生根本沒有SOP，
你才開始第一次失眠。

那個失眠不是因為事情有多大，
是因為你第一次發現「事情會失控」。

所以如果你最近睡不著，
不要罵自己玻璃心。
你只是在補上一堂
「世界本來就會出包」的課。

上完這堂課的人，
下一次遇到同樣的事，
會睡得著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個最近因為一件事失眠好幾天、開始懷疑自己抗壓性的朋友——失眠不是脆弱，是在補課
variations: 進階版：你最近睡不著的那件事，五年後回頭看會是大事還是小事？如果是小事，那今晚就睡吧——你現在的痛，是因為你還沒學會這件事很小

en.content:
There's a K-drama line that sounds like an insult
but is actually a wake-up call:
"You lost sleep over this?
Sounds like life's been pretty smooth for you so far."

First time you hear it, you're offended.
Second time, you go quiet.
Third time, you laugh —
because you realize it's true.

The biggest problem with our generation
isn't that we suffered.
It's that people who never suffered
are meeting suffering for the first time.

Well-protected as kids.
Followed the SOP through school.
Graduated thinking life had a clear next step.

Then one day —
work goes wrong, relationship breaks,
something happens at home.
You realize life has no SOP.
And you have your first sleepless night.

The insomnia isn't because the thing is huge.
It's because you're learning, for the first time,
that things can spiral.

So if you can't sleep lately,
don't call yourself weak.
You're just taking a class
you missed earlier:
"things go wrong by default."

The people who pass this class
sleep through it next time.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's lost sleep for days over one thing and is now doubting their resilience — insomnia isn't fragility, it's catching up on a missed class
variations: Advanced: the thing keeping you up — will it look big or small five years from now? If small, go to sleep tonight. Your pain comes from not yet knowing how small it is

items.sourceNote: Pinkvilla〈10 K-drama quotes we live by〉《我的出走日記》解放與崇拜命題延伸改寫

zh-tw.content:
《我的出走日記》裡有一句被很多人抄在筆記本的話：
「我從來沒有真正活過。
我只是不斷地在拒絕。
從沒愛過、也沒被真正愛過——
所以我想，至少崇拜一個人試試看。」

看起來很喪，
其實在說一件很狠的事。

大部分人活到三十歲，
都不是「沒選過自己想要的人生」，
是「一直在拒絕不想要的」。

不想要這份工作、
不想要那段關係、
不想要那種生活——
你以為這些拒絕加在一起會變成一個答案，
結果只是把生活刪到剩下一片空白。

你沒有過自己的人生，
你只是過了一個「不是別人版本」的人生。

所以這句台詞真正在問的是：
你下次說「我不想要這個」的時候，
後面那句「那我想要什麼」，
你有答案嗎？

如果還沒有，
也沒關係——
那句台詞的角色
也是從「崇拜一個人」開始找答案的。

你可以從一件小事開始崇拜：
一個你欣賞的朋友、
一個你佩服的同事、
一個你覺得活得有重量的陌生人。

崇拜不是依賴，
是讓你看見「原來人可以那樣活」。
看見了，你才知道自己也可以。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在拒絕、卻說不出自己要什麼的朋友——你不用馬上找到答案，你只需要先找到一個你想成為的樣子
variations: 進階版：今晚想一個你最近覺得「活得很扎實」的人，不用很厲害的人，只要他讓你覺得「我也想要這樣」——那個人就是你的下一個方向

en.content:
There's a line from My Liberation Notes
that people keep writing in their notebooks:
"I've never really lived.
I've just been refusing.
Never loved, never truly been loved —
so I thought, let me at least try worshipping someone."

It sounds bleak.
It's actually saying something brutal.

Most people at thirty
haven't 'chosen the life they wanted.'
They've just been refusing
the lives they didn't want.

Don't want this job.
Don't want that relationship.
Don't want that kind of life.
You thought stacking up refusals
would give you an answer.
It just edited your life down
to a blank page.

You didn't live your life.
You lived a 'not-someone-else's-version' life.

So what the line is really asking:
next time you say 'I don't want this,'
do you have an answer
to 'then what do I want'?

If you don't, that's okay too.
The character in that line
also started by 'worshipping someone'
to find the answer.

You can start small.
Worship a friend you admire.
A coworker you respect.
A stranger who seems to live with weight.

Worship isn't dependence.
It's letting yourself see
that 'a person can live that way.'
Once you see it,
you know you can too.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps refusing things but can't say what they actually want — you don't need the answer yet, you just need someone who shows you a version of life you'd want
variations: Advanced: tonight, think of one person you've recently felt 'lives with real weight.' Not someone famous, just someone who makes you think 'I'd want that too.' That person is your next direction
